summary: Team is accelerating two major report launches (China Going-Global with IE University and Davos/CES thought leadership) while launching the new Intelligent China program and selecting a social media agency, with urgent deadlines this week for agency selection, report translation, and event planning.
summaryDetail: The most critical initiative is the joint report with IE University, 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global,' which must be ready by early September for a Singapore launch mid-September. This week, Felix needs to finalize launch sessions with Tech in Asia and Tyson, and Julie must identify op-ed contributors. Simultaneously, the team is expediting social media agency selection—Media Minds is the frontrunner—and launching the Intelligent China program targeting foreigners, with Joseph preparing the website and Xuan producing a one-pager for Beijing/Shanghai events. Urgent this week: Julie must turn CES quotes into social media posters, send the Ding Hight Hall video, and adjust the school brochure; Jimmy needs to incorporate 10 points into the Shun Tian Shu press release; and the team must start SCM campaigns for Intelligent China. Upcoming: The Asia Start campaign launches with two routes (AI and traditional), and the EU-China collaboration framework with CKGSB-EUCCC is being outlined.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, school brochure updates, CES quotes for social media, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, and social media agency scouting. Concerns raised about Zhu Yang's feedback style and timeline for agency selection.

meetings.title: Joint Report Planning for 'New Chinese Playbook'
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch events at Tech in Asia and Tyson conferences in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with focus on module one in Beijing.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Menu categorization by topics is correct; no change needed.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu is needed; incorporate 10 points or convert to 10-point article.
Zhu Yang's feedback style is problematic; he may be using ChatGPT; consider limiting engagement.
Media Minds may be the best agency choice given small budget and time constraints.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of the original Chinese report.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with joint distribution.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning for 'New Chinese Playbook'

decisions.text: Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning for 'New Chinese Playbook'

decisions.text: Report to be ready by early September for launch in Singapore mid-September.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning for 'New Chinese Playbook'

decisions.text: Report will include main research from Professor Leeway, op-eds from President Santiago, Dean Hi, and possibly an industry perspective.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning for 'New Chinese Playbook'

decisions.text: Asia Start will run two parallel routes for first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will target foreigners, similar to Asia Start but more in-depth.
Go with the first title for the CES press release: 'CKGSB Professor Sun Tianshu at Summer Davos: AI’s Second Half Needs 1,000 Henry Fords'
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: CES press release - PR Newswire figures

decisions.text: Professor Li Wei is happy to collaborate with IE University on the joint report
